name: data-availability-committee
title: Data Availability Committee with Shamir Secret Sharing for Enterprise ZK Validium
hypothesis: A 3-node DAC with 2-of-3 threshold using Shamir secret sharing can attest batch data availability in under 2 seconds, with enterprise-managed storage, without exposing data to any individual node (information-theoretic privacy), and with recovery mechanism if one node fails.
null_hypothesis: Either attestation latency exceeds 2 seconds for enterprise batch sizes (100KB-1MB), or Shamir secret sharing overhead makes the protocol impractical (>10x estimated), or single-node failure prevents data recovery, or individual shares leak information about batch data.
